title: The One Where Heckles Dies

jokes.text: When she sneezed, bats flew out of them.
jokes.explanation: Strong visual absurdity with good economy - classic Chandler exaggeration style

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I mean, her nostrils are huge. You could land a helicopter in there.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A helicopter? Dude, you could land a whole squadron of fighter jets.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm breaking up with her.

jokes.text: I'm telling you, she leaned back, I could see her brain.
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but less original than the bats line - relies on similar absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line:
My nostrils aren't that big!
Okay, fine, they're huge. They're like two open car doors.

jokes.text: Joey, women don't have Adam's apples.
jokes.setup: Joey's story about girlfriend's Adam's apple
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - his confusion about basic anatomy is very much in character

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, Joey, all women have an Adam's apple.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: They do?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes! It's just smaller than men's.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh... I thought she was sick or something.

jokes.text: Heh, heh. You guys are messing with me, right?
jokes.setup: Learning women don't have Adam's apples
jokes.explanation: Standard Joey confusion beat - functional but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Women don't have Adam's apples? So what you're telling me is... they can all be spies?

jokes.text: That's a good one. For a second there, I was like, whoa!
jokes.explanation: Weak payoff - Joey's ignorance is established but resolution lacks punch

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh thank God, you guys were just messing with me!

jokes.text: Because she doesn't hate Yanni is not a real reason.
jokes.setup: Ross naming Maureen Rasillo as legitimate breakup
jokes.explanation: Good specificity with Yanni reference - captures 90s dating absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We were on a break!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What?! You slept with her?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes! But Rachel, we were on a break!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, who was it?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Maureen Rasillo.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Maureen Rasillo?! From high school?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It was one time!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Because... she said she wanted to.

jokes.text: You don't have birds. / I could have birds.
jokes.setup: Heckles complaining about disturbing his birds
jokes.punchline: Admission he doesn't have birds but could
jokes.explanation: Perfect Heckles logic - absurd but delivered with complete conviction. Establishes his eccentric character beautifully

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Heckles
dialogue.line: You people are making too much noise! You're disturbing my birds!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Mr. Heckles, you don't have any birds.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Heckles
dialogue.line: Well, I could!

jokes.text: I'm going to rejoin my dinner party.
jokes.setup: Heckles living alone and being antisocial
jokes.punchline: Pretending to have a dinner party
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Heckles' delusional social life

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Heckles
dialogue.line: I'm having a dinner party tonight.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Heckles
dialogue.line: Well, I'm pretending to have a dinner party. I'm just talking to myself.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Heckles
dialogue.line: Pass the salt, Mr. Heckles. Thank you, Mr. Heckles.

jokes.text: Hello, Chandler Bing! Oh, my God!
jokes.punchline: Spot-on Janice impression with her catchphrases
jokes.explanation: Standard Janice impression - familiar callback but well-executed

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Janice is the worst!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Why? What's wrong with her?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That laugh! 'Oh. My. God.' And the way she says everything is so annoying.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Do the impression!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh. My. God. Could he BE any more stupid? Oh. My. God!

jokes.text: Oh, Chandler! Now! Now! That's it. There! Faster!
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo disguised as Janice impression
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion - starts as character impression, becomes sexual innuendo

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: *in Janice's nasal voice* Oh. My. God.

jokes.text: We won! / Mr. Heckles.
jokes.setup: Celebrating victory over Heckles' noise complaints
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy timing - celebration immediately undercut by tragedy

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, we won! We finally got him to back off!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, I can't believe it. No more complaints, no more—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, what's that smell?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh no.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Is that... coming from downstairs?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Mr. Heckles.

jokes.text: I was sweeping yesterday. It could've been me.
jokes.punchline: Joey's illogical fear about sweeping danger
jokes.explanation: Good Joey logic - missing the real cause of death, focusing on irrelevant detail

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. Mr. Heckles died while he was sweeping?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's it. I'm never sweeping again.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's too dangerous.

jokes.text: Go into the light, Mr. Heckles!
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe spiritual nonsense delivered with complete sincerity

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Mr. Heckles? Mr. Heckles, if you're in here, knock once for yes.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, Mr. Heckles, I'm sensing a very strong presence. I'm sensing... I'm sensing that you're dead.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And I'm sensing that you're upset about something. Is it the music? Is it the noise? Is it the fact that Monica and Rachel are walking around up there in their underwear?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Mr. Heckles, I'm gonna need you to stop with the knocking, okay? You're creeping everyone out. You gotta move on. There's a light, Mr. Heckles. Go into the light!

jokes.text: Like crop circles or the Bermuda Triangle or evolution.
jokes.setup: Listing things she doesn't believe in
jokes.punchline: Including established scientific fact with conspiracy theories
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - puts science on same level as pseudoscience, very Phoebe

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I don't believe in the moon. I think it's just the back of the sun.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Also, I don't believe in dinosaurs. I think they're a hoax made up by the government.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And I don't believe in Canada. It's just a giant snow globe.

jokes.text: Monkeys, Darwin, it's a nice story. I just think it's a little too easy.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion - calling millions of years of evolution 'too easy'. Perfect Phoebe logic

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You don't believe in evolution?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, I don't believe in it.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You don't believe in evolution?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, not as a literal concept, okay? Because, I mean, you weren't there. You don't know.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I wasn't there when they took the pyramids, but... I'm pretty sure they're not just a lot of sand.

jokes.text: Okay, don't get me started on gravity.
jokes.setup: Ross invoking gravity as undeniable fact
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and economy - one line sets up entire absurd worldview. Highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Gravity is not just a theory, it's a law.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I don't believe in it.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You don't believe in it?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No. If it were real, wouldn't all the people in China just fall off?

jokes.text: Lately I get the feeling that I'm not so much being pulled down as I am being pushed.
jokes.punchline: Alternative gravity theory with mysterious pushers
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific absurdity - Phoebe has thought this through in her own way

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I don't believe in gravity.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You don't believe in gravity?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No. I think things just fall down because there are people under the earth going 'push' really hard.

jokes.text: Uh-oh. It's Isaac Newton and he's pissed.
jokes.setup: Knock at door during gravity discussion
jokes.punchline: Perfect timing reference to gravity's discoverer
jokes.explanation: Excellent timing and reference - perfect Chandler quip with great economy

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more affected by gravity right now?

jokes.text: According my client's will, he wants to leave all his earthly possessions to 'the noisy girls in the apartment above mine.'
jokes.punchline: Heckles' backhanded description of Monica and Rachel
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established dynamic with nice character touch

dialogue.speaker: Lawyer
dialogue.line: To Monica Geller and Rachel Green, I leave my entire collection of fine art and antiques, as well as my prized possessions. These items go to two women of taste, beauty, and the good sense to keep their stereo at a reasonable volume.

jokes.text: All right, there was none.
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection but effective - quick deflation of expectations

dialogue.line:
Let's talk money

Immediate deflation - no money

jokes.text: He hated us! This was his final revenge! / Ever seen so much crap! Actually, I think this apartment sullies the good name of crap.
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler voice with creative escalation - 'sullies the good name of crap' is clever

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not sure what's more depressing - that he lives like this, or that I'm pretty sure my apartment looks exactly like this.

jokes.text: So now the real question is, who put those fossils there and why?
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - accepts evidence but creates bigger conspiracy. Character-perfect

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: See, these fossils prove that dinosaurs existed millions of years ago.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Ok, but like, how do we know the government didn't just bury them there to mess with us?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: The government didn't bury dinosaur fossils, Phoebe.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's exactly what they'd want you to think!

jokes.text: April 18th, excessive noise, Italian guy's gay roommate brings home dry cleaning.
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Heckles' obsessive documentation of normal activities

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: "9:30 AM - heard footsteps above my apartment. Concerned they might be human." "10:15 AM - yes, confirmed human." "2:00 PM - heard more footsteps. Possibly same human. Getting to know their schedule." "3:45 PM - human is definitely active. Have named him Steve."

jokes.text: You don't have any stuff. / You still think of it as your apartment, don't you?
jokes.explanation: Good character conflict moment - Monica's controlling nature vs Rachel's assertion

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You can't just move my things around! This apartment is a reflection of my taste and organization.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, it's not a museum. It's where we live. And honestly, your 'perfect' arrangement looks like a furniture catalog threw up in here.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, maybe if you had any sense of style, you'd understand—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, I understand perfectly. I understand that you need to control everything because you're terrified that if something's even slightly out of place, your entire world falls apart. News flash: it won't.

jokes.text: Maybe the Overlords needed them to steer their spacecrafts.
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific Phoebe worldview - 'Overlords' is a great touch

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So you're saying aliens gave us opposable thumbs?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, yeah. I mean, how else would we have gotten them? It's not like we just evolved them naturally or something.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Actually, Phoebe, that's exactly how we got them. Through evolution over millions of years.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh sure, that's what they want you to think.

jokes.text: I think maybe it's time you put Ross under the microscope.
jokes.punchline: Scientific metaphor turned back on Ross
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay using Ross's own scientific language against him

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, come on Ross. Evolution is just a theory.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's a scientific theory, Phoebe. There's a difference.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, and you're a theory. A theory that no one asked for.

jokes.text: Wow. Heckles was voted class clown, and so was I.
jokes.explanation: Sets up major character arc for episode - Chandler's existential crisis

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Look at this picture of him in high school. He was a loner, he had no friends, nobody liked him.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: He used to sit in the cafeteria by himself... eating lunch alone... reading alone...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, I'm looking at the wrong yearbook. This is mine.

jokes.text: Well, there was no club, but I sure thought they were cool.
jokes.setup: Scale modeler's club connection with Heckles
jokes.explanation: Good character depth moment - shows Chandler's social isolation tendency

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I was in the scale modelers' club in high school. Mr. Heckles was the faculty advisor.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I was very lonely.

jokes.text: So? You were both dorks. Big deal.
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey missing the point completely - good character contrast

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I'm turning into my father.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? No you're not.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes I am. I'm getting older, I'm turning into him.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So what? My father was a dancer. It's not the worst thing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Your father was a Chippendales dancer, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, and he was great!

jokes.text: Vivian, too tall. / Madge, big gums. / Too loud. / Too smart. / Makes noise when she eats.
jokes.setup: Looking at Heckles' photos and notes
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Chandler's future - each complaint more petty than last

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Look at this. 'She chewed too loud.' 'She had a loud laugh.' 'She wore her hair up.' What is wrong with this guy?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Keep reading.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: 'She wore her hair down.' 'She wore her hair half-up, half-down.'

jokes.text: Bitter Town, Alone-ville, Hermit Junction!
jokes.setup: Train metaphor for his life path
jokes.punchline: Creative place names for lonely destinations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay with great rhythm - highly quotable Chandler line

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: My life is like a train with no destination. I'm just riding through lonely stations.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: There's 'Desperation Station,' 'Regret Ridge,' and my personal favorite: 'Could I BE any more lost?'

jokes.text: What if I found her but I dumped her because she pronounces it 'supposably'?
jokes.punchline: Specific grammar pet peeve as relationship ender
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler neurosis - very specific and relatable pickiness

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm afraid of dying alone.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're not going to die alone.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How do you know? What if I never find someone?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, you'll find someone. You're a great guy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, but what if they use the word 'supposably'? I couldn't be with someone who says 'supposably' instead of 'supposedly.' I'd have to break up with them.

jokes.text: I mean, what if we're over at her folks' place?
jokes.explanation: Realistic but uncomfortable moment - Joey's practical concern vs friendship

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So, are you guys doing anything for Thanksgiving?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm going to my parents' place.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, that sounds nice. Maybe I could—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, no, that's not gonna work. My mom's already got the seating chart done, and she specifically said no extra people because last year my Uncle Frank got drunk and fell into the TV.

jokes.text: Supposably. / Supposably. / Did they go to the zoo? / Supposably.
jokes.punchline: Obsessive repetition of grammar pet peeve
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Chandler's obsessive nature - practice makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more obsessive about grammar? Could I BE any more obsessive about grammar? Could I BE any more obsessive about grammar?

jokes.text: Jeez, look how fat she got.
jokes.explanation: Typical shallow Chandler reaction but lacks originality - setup for bigger reveal

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh. My. God.

jokes.text: Is it yours? Ha! You wish, Chandler Bing.
jokes.explanation: Good Janice character moment - appropriately dismissive of Chandler

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So how's the baby?

dialogue.speaker: Janice
dialogue.line: Oh, he's fine.

jokes.text: And what? Miss the expression on your face? Oh, no. Janice likes to have her fun.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking why she didn't tell him on phone
jokes.punchline: Janice admitting she enjoyed his pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Janice cruelty - she knows exactly what she's doing to Chandler

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why didn't you tell me on the phone?

dialogue.speaker: Janice
dialogue.line: Because I enjoyed hearing the pain in your voice.

jokes.text: Then you'll just have to eat the other lamps.
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay attempt - doesn't land clearly, seems forced

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're allergic to shellfish? Since when?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Since I ate that shrimp last week and my throat closed up.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, that was a lamp.

jokes.text: And up till 50 years ago, you all thought the atom was the smallest thing until you split it open and this whole mess of crap came out.
jokes.punchline: Describing atomic particles as 'mess of crap'
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe voice - reduces complex physics to her terminology

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, nothing is certain, you know? Like, atoms? They're just, like, a mess of crap.

jokes.text: There might be a teeny, tiny possibility.
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - scientist forced to acknowledge uncertainty

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: See, I don't believe in evolution. I think that man was just placed here, with all the other animals.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, Phoebe, I'm not gonna waste my time trying to convince you that evolution is real.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Because you can't!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, because I can't.

jokes.text: How are you gonna face the other science guys?
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler observation - 'science guys' is appropriately casual

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm sorry, I didn't realize that 'science guys' were a protected class. We can't say anything bad about them? What if one of them kept me up all night talking about the Krebs cycle?

jokes.text: I was putting on my jacket and the thing and the lamp and it broke.
jokes.explanation: Standard bad lying comedy - functional but unremarkable

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, so what happened was... I was doing my aerobics, right? And I was jumping around, and I kicked my leg up really high, and I accidentally hit the lamp!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Rachel, you broke it like three days ago.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No I didn't!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes you did. I saw you. You threw a pillow at Monica and it knocked over the lamp.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That was... a different lamp.

jokes.text: I didn't see it, because I was putting on my jacket. But I want to believe you.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe diplomacy - wants to be supportive but can't quite lie

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's... nice.

jokes.text: Like that guy on the subway who eats his own face.
jokes.setup: Needing a 'thing' as lonely old man
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and disturbing - classic NYC weird character reference

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I need a thing. You know, a thing that defines me. Some people have a thing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You have a thing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? What is my thing?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I don't know. Your thing is... you're gonna end up alone, like that creepy old man in Washington Square Park who smells like hot dogs.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, that's not a thing, that's just a lifestyle.

jokes.text: Run away from Crazy Snake Man, they'll shout!
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the snake man fantasy - Chandler's self-deprecating vision

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm going to have snakes. Lots of snakes. My kids are going to come home from school, open their lunch boxes, and find a cobra.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: They'll be like, 'Dad, why is there a snake in my sandwich?' And I'll be like, 'Because I'm cool, that's why.'

jokes.text: Chandler, you have just described virtually every man that we've ever gone out with.
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing out universality of male behavior
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about dating patterns - relatable and true

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, I know. I'm commitment-phobic, I'm emotionally unavailable, I make inappropriate jokes at uncomfortable times...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So you're a man.

jokes.text: Whoa! Don't know about that.
jokes.setup: Friends saying he's ready for commitment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate panic at the word 'commitment'
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler commitment phobia - predictable but in character

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm ready for commitment.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh God, what have I done?

jokes.text: Clock whistles
jokes.explanation: Simple physical gag - immediate consequence for Monica's acceptance

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, I love it! Thank you so much!

dialogue.speaker: Sound effect
dialogue.line: *BEEP BEEP BEEP BEEP*

jokes.text: But I'm not going to let that stuff hang me up anymore because she has an unusually large head.
jokes.setup: Chandler announcing growth and new date
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Chandler hasn't learned - says he's growing while demonstrating he hasn't

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I've really grown as a person. I'm going on a date with this amazing woman.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: But I'm worried she's going to think my shoes are stupid.

jokes.text: You're all going to hell.
jokes.setup: Everyone laughing during Heckles memorial moment
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe spiritual moment - appropriate moral judgment delivery

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I can sense his presence here. He's telling me he's very happy. He says you all treated him very well... except for you, Monica. He says you used to stomp around up there like an elephant in high heels.

jokes.text: My God, that's a big head! It didn't look this big in the office. Maybe it's the lighting. My head must look like a golf ball at work.
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Chandler's obsessive mind - the golf ball comparison is inspired

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So, uh, how's the, uh... how's the weather been treating that big head of yours?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I mean, you must need SPF 1000 just to get a sunburn. You could be a satellite.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I bet you have to special order hats. Do they even make visors that big?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could you turn sideways? I want to see if you're one of those optical illusions or if your head is actually that large.

jokes.text: Nice smile, good dresser. Big head, big head, big head!
jokes.punchline: Obsession overwhelming his attempt at positivity
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of Chandler's inability to move past physical flaws - great rhythm

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm a great listener, I'm loyal, I'm funny, I'm... Oh God, I'm obsessed with you.

tmdb_overview: After Mr. Heckles dies and leaves all his belongings to Monica and Rachel, Chandler discovers eerie similarities between the late eccentric man and his own life. Ross and Phoebe have a dispute over the progression of evolution.
insight: Steady character work carries an emotionally ambitious episode despite uneven pacing.
